perturbation.original_text: 1.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Todos hereby grants the Reseller a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, exclusive right to distribute and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory; provided, however, that Reseller may sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller. The Reseller shall have a right of first refusal to include within this Agreement any additional products developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company following the Effective Date that are not currently included in Exhibit A, and upon the exercise of such right, the term "Products" shall be expanded to mean such additional products as well. For purposes of clarity, the parties agree that upon Todos's development of a blood screening test for colon cancer, such product shall be added to this Agreement and included within the definition of "Products", subject to the Reseller and Todos agreeing on the commercial terms for such product, including the price.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Todos hereby grants the Reseller a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, exclusive right to distribute and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory; provided, however, that Reseller may sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller. The Reseller shall have a right of first refusal to include within this Agreement any additional products developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company following the Effective Date that are not currently included in Exhibit A, and upon the exercise of such right, the term "Products" shall be expanded to mean such additional products as well.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'For purposes of clarity, the parties agree that upon Todos's development of a blood screening test for colon cancer, such product shall be added to this Agreement and included within the definition of "Products", subject to the Reseller and Todos agreeing on the commercial terms for such product, including the price.', there's no agreement on how to add a new product within the definition of "Products". This omission contradicts section 2.1 which refers to milestones required for the Reseller's exclusive right, the lack of clarity makes it harder to determine if such milestones apply to new products such as the blood screening test for colon cancer. This contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement because it is not clear if milestones are inclusive of the new product. This could potentially impact the Reseller's exclusive right based on whether the new product is considered in evaluating milestones.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Reseller's exclusive right to market and sell the Products in the Territory is subject to the Reseller achieving the following milestones by the end of each year this Agreement is in effect (the "Annual Milestones"): Year Annual Milestone(s) Year 1 Not Applicable Each Year Thereafter The parties will agree at the beginning of the year on the Annual Milestone for such year
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence regarding the blood screening test for colon cancer creates a direct contradiction. The original text clarified the inclusion process for new products within the definition of 'Products.' By removing this clarification, the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding whether the milestones in section 2.1 apply to the new product. Section 2.1 links the Reseller's exclusive right to achieving certain milestones. The lack of clarity on whether these milestones include the new blood screening product directly impacts the enforceability and interpretation of the Reseller's exclusive right, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction by removing a specific example of the 'Products' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 The Reseller shall be responsible for setting up at least one laboratory in the Territory to support the assay protocol (the "Laboratory"), including the provision of a FTIR that is approved by Todos, as further described in Exhibit B. The Reseller shall obtain the prior approval of Todos for all lab equipment. The Reseller will contract with existing certified laboratories in Israel to obtain the blood samples data, subject to the approval by Todos of each such laboratory.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 The Reseller shall be responsible for setting up at least one laboratory in the Territory to support the assay protocol (the "Laboratory"), including the provision of a FTIR that is approved by Todos. The Reseller will contract with existing certified laboratories in Israel to obtain the blood samples data, subject to the approval by Todos of each such laboratory.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The Reseller shall obtain the prior approval of Todos for all lab equipment', the obligations regarding lab equipment are not well defined in section 4.4. This contradicts exhibit B which requires description of the laboratory and its components. This creates uncertainty in enforcement, as there is no requirement of Reseller, for acquiring the prior approval of Todos, for all lab equipment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT B THE LABORATORY [please insert description of the laboratory and its components]
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly required Todos' prior approval for all lab equipment. The changed text removes this requirement, directly contradicting Exhibit B which requires a detailed description of the laboratory and its components. Without Todos' approval, the lab setup might not meet the standards implied by Exhibit B, leading to a strong contradiction regarding the required specifications and approval process for the laboratory equipment.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Product Price. The Reseller shall be entitled to purchase the Products from Todos for resale to Customers at a price between US$[ ] and US$[ ], with the actual price to be agreed upon by the Parties (the "Product Price"). At the end of each year this Agreement is in effect, the Parties will discuss each party's costs and whether to revise the Product Price. Todos shall provide the Reseller with Products for clinical trials at no charge.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Product Price. The Reseller shall be entitled to purchase the Products from Todos for resale to Customers at a price between US$[ ] and US$[ ], with the actual price to be agreed upon by the Parties (the "Product Price"). Todos shall provide the Reseller with Products for clinical trials at no charge.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'At the end of each year this Agreement is in effect, the Parties will discuss each party's costs and whether to revise the Product Price.', there's no agreement on how to revise product price within the text. This omission contradicts section 2.1 which refers to milestones required for the Reseller's exclusive right, the lack of an established procedure makes it harder to determine the correct product price. This contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement because the price may not reflect the actual cost.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Reseller's exclusive right to market and sell the Products in the Territory is subject to the Reseller achieving the following milestones by the end of each year this Agreement is in effect (the "Annual Milestones"): Year Annual Milestone(s) Year 1 Not Applicable Each Year Thereafter The parties will agree at the beginning of the year on the Annual Milestone for such year
perturbation.justification: The original text had a process for revising the product price annually. The changed text removes this process. Section 2.1 states that the parties will agree on the annual milestones at the beginning of the year. Because the changed text removes the section regarding the revision of product price, this creates an in-text contradiction because the milestone requirements might not reflect the actual costs anymore, which are supposed to be reflected in the revision of product price in the original text. The removal of the price revision process makes it more difficult to negotiate annual milestones, thus creating a contradiction.
